The parents of a 13-year-old Italian boy are facing manslaughter charges after their son was accused of killing a tourist by throwing a heavy statuette from a balcony. Chiara Jaconis, 30, was celebrating her birthday in Naples with her boyfriend in 2024 when she was fatally struck by a 2kg figure that was thrown from above as she walked down the street.  The teen, who was accused of throwing the object, was cleared by a juvenile court due to his age, but prosecutors now argue that Chiara's death could have been prevented if the boy's parents had kept a closer eye on him.

British widow Janet Fordham, 69, from Devon, died in a car crash in Ghana after she was conned by romance fraudsters out of her entire £1million life savings, an inquest has heard. Devon Coroner's Court heard Mrs Fordham had fallen victim to several online scams over a five-year period that saw her lose her home and life savings. She suffered fatal injuries in the collision on February 14, 2023 having travelled to the country the previous October.

A four-year-old girl had her birthday party ruined after two balaclava-clad teenagers destroyed a wooden stall she uses to sell eggs from outside her home. Maisie Willis would regularly sell eggs from her chickens to passersby on her street in Holbury, near Southampton. However, she was left heartbroken after two teenage boys wearing balaclavas entered her garden and took off with the wooden stand, which was still full of eggs, while she was celebrating her fourth birthday. The stand was found smashed into pieces on a nearby road, with the eggs thrown all over the street.

A British adventurer faces going blind after contracting a potentially deadly infection while trying to visit every country in the world. David Simpson, 38, had already visited 150 countries before picking up latent tuberculosis in South Asia. The Belfast local said the illness began attacking his left eye, causing flashing lights and blurred vision and forcing him onto powerful medication and immunosuppressants as doctors tried to stop it getting worse.

Kung-fu, half marathons and now ping pong - is there anything robots can't do? An AI robot called Ace has pulled off wins against three elite table tennis players. Developed by Sony AI, the robot is fully autonomous, and uses a complex array of vision sensors, control systems and high-speed hardware to repsond in real-time. Incredible footage shows the bot winning three out of five matches against elite players - showing off sophisticated moves including unusual spins and bouncing balls off the net.

Does it really matter, a little bit of fun for themselves??

A group of Finnish air force cadets have been reprimanded after they were caught drawing penises in the sky with their flight patterns. The student pilots were flying two Grob G115 training aircraft from Jyväskylä on the morning of April 13, when analysis of their route on the flight-tracking website Flightradar showed that their manoeuvres resembled phallic shapes. The air cadets were also found to have drawn other shapes, such as hearts.
  • 77 comments
